title: Music as Universal Language Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A situation in which there is a lot of damage or confusion.
choices: havoc
render
gait
rounds.answer: havoc
rounds.explanation: havoc: A situation in which there is a lot of damage or confusion.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Not showing respect for other people; rude or disrespectful.
choices: havoc
render
impudent
rounds.answer: impudent
rounds.explanation: impudent: Not showing respect for other people; rude or disrespectful.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To become real or to appear after being imagined or planned.
choices: render
materialize
havoc
rounds.answer: materialize
rounds.explanation: materialize: To become real or to appear after being imagined or planned.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Skilful and quick in movement or action.
rounds.answer: deft

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Acting quickly without thinking about the consequences.
rounds.answer: impulsive

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To understand or grasp something; to catch someone who has done wrong.
rounds.answer: apprehend

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: She has a graceful gait when she dances across the stage.
The impudent student talked back to the teacher during class.
He ran headlong into the room, excited to see his friends.

rounds.explanation: The gymnast amazed everyone with her incredible contortion skills during the show.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1: Role-Play Interview with a Performer
rounds.target: In this activity, one person plays the role of a journalist and the other plays the role of a young performer who has just won a competition.
The impudent student talked back to the teacher during class.
Explain one important unit idea, give a reason with because, and respond to two follow-up questions.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The plan to build a new park will materialize next year.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The math answers were accurate, so she got an excellent grade.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: If you copy your friend's essay without asking, that is plagiarism.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'vulnerable' mean in this unit?
choices: Easily hurt or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
A situation in which there is a lot of damage or confusion.
To make something become a certain way; to perform or deliver.
rounds.answer: Easily hurt or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
rounds.explanation: vulnerable: Easily hurt or harmed, either physically or emotionally.

rounds.prompt: What does 'succumb' mean in this unit?
choices: A situation in which there is a lot of damage or confusion.
To make something become a certain way; to perform or deliver.
To give in to something or to stop resisting, often because it is too strong.
rounds.answer: To give in to something or to stop resisting, often because it is too strong.
rounds.explanation: succumb: To give in to something or to stop resisting, often because it is too strong.

rounds.answer: To make something become a certain way; to perform or deliver.

games.title: Memory Pairs
